title: cuscuta andina
intro: Cuscuta andina, also known as the Andean dodder, is a type of parasitic plant that derives its nutrients by connecting to the stems of other plants, often referred to as a hemiparasite. Traditionally, Cuscuta andina has been used in folk medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. The plant has been reported to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting the immune system. In herbal preparations, Cuscuta andina can be found in teas, infusions, and capsules, although its effectiveness and safety as a medicinal plant require further scientific research and validation.
